headline: Stateless children exceed 12,000 in Sabah alone
context: A 2025 study by Sabah-based NGOs estimated over 12,000 stateless children in the state, many born to undocumented parents.

cards.big: 12,000 stateless children in Sabah denied birth certificates and identity
cards.sub: Without citizenship, they cannot access healthcare or education

cards.big: Federal Constitution Article 14 grants citizenship by birth under conditions
cards.sub: Stateless children fall through gaps in jus soli and jus sanguinis provisions

cards.big: Malaysia has not ratified the 1961 Convention on Reduction of Statelessness
cards.sub: Suhakam recommended ratification in its 2023 annual report

cards.big: Over 60% of stateless children in Sabah are of Bajau Laut descent
cards.sub: Sea nomad communities have faced documentation barriers for generations

cards.big: Are 12,000 children invisible because of paperwork or political will?
NRD processed only 340 late birth registrations from Sabah in 2024
